name: Equicourt
description: An AI-powered micro-litigation platform that resolves petty disputes by analyzing evidence from text, PDFs, images, and live speech via speech recognition, then applies Canadian law through a RAG-enabled multi-LLM system to deliver grounded verdicts.

name: UofT ClubHub
description: A platform created to enhance student engagement and simplify access to clubs, making it easier for students to stay involved in campus life - featuring clubs/posts search functionalities, management system for club executives, automatic Instagram scraper, and AI chatbot.

name: PseudoGrader
description: A website that validates and tests pseudocode from text, images, or PDFs, using AI-generated logic checks, code translation, and test case evaluation, streamlining feedback for students and educators.

name: Basic Neural Network
description: A minimalistic and customizable neural network built from scratch using Python and NumPy that achieved 95% accuracy on the MNIST dataset with custom hyperparameters.

name: Scriptorium
description: An interactive web app that allows users to write, execute, and save code in various programming languages and create and comment on blog posts based on these code templates.

name: WelcoMate (UofT W3B Davinci Competition B2B 1st Place Winner)
description: A website offering an all-in-one hotel PMS and CRM system with an AI-powered trip planner, where each guest can check-in, book services, and get personalized trip plans.

name: Little Learners
description: An educational web app designed for youth with learning disabilities, combining a structured English learning roadmap, engaging multi-sensory mini-games, rewards, and personalized AI-generated books.

name: Toronto Asian Art Museum Explorer
description: An Android app that showcases museum artifacts from a database, with searching and filtering features, administration tools (addition and removal of items), and PDF report generation.

name: League of Legends Statistics Analyzer
description: Collected 100k data entries from relevant websites and the Riot API on team compositions and other statistics, built a machine-learning model with over 75% accuracy, and developed a website for the model and display of player statistics.

name: MealSimple (Uoft EWB Hackathon 1st Place Winner)
description: A website that uses the Google Maps API to locate food banks, a recipes API to filter recipes by ingredients, and the ChatGPT API to generate custom recipes based on user preferences.

name: Daily World Diary
description: Based on popular news stories of the day, using the ChatGPT API, generates a 'world diary' written in first person with captivating text and cited sources.

name: Broke Besties
description: A web application that uses AI-powered receipt scanning and natural language interactions to help users accurately track, split, and manage shared expenses and debts with friends and groups.
